title: Mapping the literature
text: tf_litmap() computes three things records. counts keyword co-occurrence within records, groups co-occurring keywords thematic components, counts reference co-citation. edge kept co-occurrence count reaches min_link (default 2), suppresses incidental pairings. Records read file order every result sorted, map fully deterministic. thematic components connected groups co-occurring keywords. fixture constructed threshold-two graph separates cleanly four themes. Co-citation computed way references field. Two cited sources linked least min_link records corpus cite together.
code: lm <- tf_litmap(corpus, min_link = 2)  lm$n_records #> [1] 8 unlist(lm$keywords)                 # the vocabulary of the corpus #> [1] "appraisal"                      "arousal"                        #> [3] "avoidance"                      "catastrophic misinterpretation" #> [5] "exposure"                       "genetics"                       #> [7] "heritability"                   "interoception" lm$keyword_cooccurrence[[1]]        # first kept co-occurrence edge #> $a #> [1] "appraisal" #>  #> $b #> [1] "catastrophic misinterpretation" #>  #> $count #> [1] 2 for (theme in lm$themes) {   cat(theme$id, ":", paste(unlist(theme$keywords), collapse = ", "), "\n") } #> theme_1 : appraisal, catastrophic misinterpretation  #> theme_2 : arousal, interoception  #> theme_3 : avoidance, exposure  #> theme_4 : genetics, heritability lm$co_citation #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$a #> [1] "barlow2002" #>  #> [[1]]$b #> [1] "clark1986" #>  #> [[1]]$count #> [1] 3 #>  #>  #> [[2]] #> [[2]]$a #> [1] "bouton2001" #>  #> [[2]]$b #> [1] "craske2008" #>  #> [[2]]$count #> [1] 2

text: tf_landscape() maps theory’s focal constructs registered alternatives onto themes tf_litmap(). theme tagged "under_theorised" neither focal theory alternative touches , "covered" exactly one , "crowded" two . Matching shared tokens theme keywords text construct labels, theory title alternatives. build small theory whose constructs speak arousal appraisal, register one alternative also speaks appraisal. leaves arousal theme covered, appraisal theme crowded remaining themes untouched. theme result records alternatives land , whether focal theory lands , resulting status. Two summaries draw practical reading. -theorised fronts themes field around theory yet engaged, candidates new work. redundancy risk lists crowded themes, new contribution distinguish existing accounts.
code: theory <- tf_theory("panic-appraisal", "Arousal and appraisal in panic") |>   tf_add_construct("c_arousal", "Physiological arousal",                    "Bodily activation in response to a stressor.") |>   tf_add_construct("c_appraisal", "Threat appraisal",                    "Catastrophic appraisal of bodily sensations.") |>   tf_add_alternative("alt_cog", "Cognitive appraisal account",                      key_constructs = c("appraisal"))  landscape <- tf_landscape(theory, corpus, min_link = 2) landscape$theory_id #> [1] "panic-appraisal" for (theme in landscape$themes) {   cat(theme$id,       "| status:", theme$status,       "| focal:", theme$focal,       "| alternatives:", paste(unlist(theme$alternatives), collapse = ", "),       "\n") } #> theme_1 | status: crowded | focal: TRUE | alternatives: alt_cog  #> theme_2 | status: covered | focal: TRUE | alternatives:   #> theme_3 | status: under_theorised | focal: FALSE | alternatives:   #> theme_4 | status: under_theorised | focal: FALSE | alternatives: unlist(landscape$under_theorised_fronts) #> [1] "theme_3" "theme_4" unlist(landscape$redundancy_risk) #> [1] "theme_1"

code: theory <- tf_theory("panic-network", "A network theory of panic") |>   tf_add_construct("c_arousal", "Physiological arousal",                    "Bodily activation in response to a stressor.",                    measurement = "heart rate variability",                    boundary_conditions = "awake adults") |>   tf_add_construct("c_threat", "Perceived threat",                    "Appraised danger of bodily sensations.",                    measurement = "self-report appraisal scale",                    boundary_conditions = "awake adults") |>   tf_add_proposition("p1", "c_arousal", "c_threat", "causes",                      mechanism = "Activation raises the salience of threat cues.") |>   tf_add_prediction("h1", "Arousal raises threat appraisal by a fixed amount.",                     "point", derives_from = "p1")  isTRUE(tf_validate(theory)) # structural checks: required fields and enums #> [1] TRUE isTRUE(tf_validate(theory, full = TRUE)) # also checks referential integrity of ids and cross-references #> [1] TRUE broken <- theory broken$predictions[[1]]$derives_from <- "p_missing" tf_validate(broken, full = TRUE) #> Error: #> ! invalid theory object: prediction[0] derives_from 'p_missing' is not a known proposition extended <- theory |>   tf_add_assumption("a1", "Arousal is measured at rest.", added_for = "h1") |>   tf_set_formal_model("sem", spec_ref = "panic-sem.lavaan") do.call(rbind, lapply(extended$provenance, as.data.frame)) #>   step              action        detail #> 1    1           tf_theory panic-network #> 2    2    tf_add_construct     c_arousal #> 3    3    tf_add_construct      c_threat #> 4    4  tf_add_proposition            p1 #> 5    5   tf_add_prediction            h1 #> 6    6   tf_add_assumption            a1 #> 7    7 tf_set_formal_model           sem path <- tempfile(fileext = ".yaml") tf_write(theory, path) roundtrip <- tf_read(path) identical(roundtrip$id, theory$id) #> [1] TRUE

code: theory <- tf_theory("demo-1", "A demonstration theory") |>   tf_add_construct("c_arousal", "Arousal", "bodily activation") |>   tf_add_construct("c_threat", "Threat", "appraised danger") # A toy deterministic embedder: bag-of-words counts over a fixed vocabulary. vocab <- c("bodily", "activation", "appraised", "danger") embedder <- function(def) {   words <- strsplit(tolower(def), "\\s+")[[1]]   vapply(vocab, function(w) sum(words == w), numeric(1)) } tf_embedding_redundancy(theory, embedder) #>           a        b cosine flag #> 1 c_arousal c_threat      0   ok

title: Simulate a theory's construct network as a linear dynamical system — tf_simulate

title: Validate a theory object — tf_validate
code: theory <- tf_read(system.file("fixtures", "panic-network.theory.yaml",                               package = "theoryforge")) isTRUE(tf_validate(theory))              # required fields and enums #> [1] TRUE isTRUE(tf_validate(theory, full = TRUE)) # also ids and cross-references #> [1] TRUE  # The failure path is the more informative one. Point a prediction at a # proposition that was never declared. broken <- theory broken$predictions[[1]]$derives_from <- "p_missing" tryCatch(tf_validate(broken, full = TRUE), error = conditionMessage) #> [1] "invalid theory object: prediction[0] derives_from 'p_missing' is not a known proposition"
